author_headline: Senior ML Engineer @PayPal | SDE @Microsoft | Marketer | 325k+ @Linkedin | 75k+ @Insta | 20k+ @X | GenAI, Agentic AI, MCP | 10k+ @Whatsapp | ISB | Mentor @Scaler | SDE, AI, Tech, Data Content Creator | DM for Collabs
content:
You will earn ₹30 lakh over the next 5 years at your current LPA. He will earn ₹1.8 crore. ₹1.5 crore gap. Same college. Same degree. Same week of graduation.

And the math runs in his favour every single month, on autopilot: appraisals, bonuses, ESOPs, the compounding of starting at 30 LPA instead of 6, while you keep restarting DSA sheets, then say "next month I'll do it properly."

Here is what the 30 LPA side actually built before placements:

a) 50 DSA patterns mapped to recognition triggers
b) LLD fluency & HLD basics
d) GenAI fundamentals
e) Mock interviews under pressure
f) Actual job applications: referrals, follow-ups, conversion tracked weekly

The gap was never "he is smarter."
The gap was "he ran a full system. You ran a sheet."

1500+ students are using the SDE Career Tracker to run exactly that system.

₹1.5 crore over 5 years. Decided in the 14 weeks before placements, not the 4 years inside them.

The maths is already running.
The only question is in whose favour.

author_headline: Software Engineer @Delhivery | 100K+ LinkedIn | Helping Brands Go Viral Organically | Brand Partnerships | AI, Tech & Marketing | DM for collabs
content:
From "Bhai, aaj wali post pe 5 likes bhi nahi aaye"... to a community of 100K+ people learning and growing together on LinkedIn.

If someone had told me this a few years ago, I probably wouldn't have believed them. Because this journey wasn't built by one viral post.

It was built by showing up, again and again. There were days when my posts barely reached anyone.

When I started creating content on LinkedIn, my goal was the same as most people: build a personal brand, get referrals, create more career opportunities for myself.

But somewhere along the way, that goal changed. I realized my content was helping people beyond just likes and impressions - every interview experience, every roadmap, every resource, every cheat sheet, every job opportunity, every lesson from my own journey.

Today, this community has grown to 100K+, and I genuinely believe this milestone belongs to all of us.

Thank you to everyone who has read a post, left a comment, shared my content, sent feedback, or simply supported me silently. A special thank you to Khushbu th for being an incredible part of this journey.

100K is just the beginning. Let's build the next 100K together.

author_headline: Co-Founder& CEO at Skyroot Aerospace | Forbes 30-Under-30 Asia | IIT-Kgp | Launched India's first private rocket to Space | Always looking for amazing people to join Team Skyroot-InMail me your resume.
content:
What a day. One for the history books.

A dream launch to orbit, followed by the most memorable conversation with Hon'ble Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi.

Deeply grateful to Modi ji for watching the launch livestream with such excitement and for making the first call to congratulate Team @SkyrootA as Vikram-1 successfully launched satellites into orbit on its maiden attempt.

Every word from him was a powerful vote of confidence in India's young engineers, entrepreneurs and dreamers.

A day I will never forget.

Onwards and upwards from here.

#Vikram1 #MissionAagaman #MissionAccomplished

author_headline: Ex-SWE Intern @ MasterCard | Expert @ Codeforces | Knight @ Leetcode | Amazon ML Summer School'25 | Final Year Student @ Nirma University
content:
Excited to share that our team earned 1st place globally in Mastercard's 2026 Global Intern Innovation Challenge.

Competing against 36 teams and 653 interns from around the world, we developed MasterGuard, a solution designed to help people better recognize, prevent, and respond to fraud and scams.

One of the most rewarding aspects of this experience was collaborating with an incredible team across different countries and time zones. It was inspiring to learn from one another, build on each other's ideas, and create something we're all proud of.

A special thank you to our mentor Gordon Adam for his guidance throughout the challenge, and to Mastercard for creating opportunities that encourage innovation, collaboration, and learning.

author_headline: E-learning nonprofit - Learn to code. Build projects. Earn certifications.
content: When you're looking for a new book to read, getting suggestions from a friend can be helpful. Well, in this course, you'll build your very own book recommender. You'll use an LLM and Python to build the engine and learn about vector search, zero-shot classification, sentiment analysis, and more.

author_headline: Junior Analyst @ JPMC || BITS Pilani Goa 26'
content:
With no background in finance, I randomly stepped into this journey and I'm so grateful to have grabbed the opportunity the moment it came my way.

6 months have passed by and learning never stops here. The firm and the people ensure you keep growing with each task you complete. I'm so grateful to everyone who took out the time from their hectic schedules to teach me and help me fit better. Working alongside the GCB EMEA team has been such a pleasure.

Never in my college life did I imagine I could step into finance having taken no related courses. Yet here I am understanding businesses, building decks, indulging in deals, and attending client calls. What's important in the process is that you never stop learning, you never limit yourself and life takes you places.

I'm deeply grateful to my family, my friends, and my professors who have always given me the confidence to dream bigger in life!

I'm thrilled to be joining JPMorganChase as a full-time Junior Analyst! Looking back, this journey is a reminder that some of the best opportunities come from taking a chance on something unfamiliar. As I step into this next chapter, I hope to keep learning, keep growing, and contributing alongside some of the brightest minds in the industry.

author_headline: SWE Intern @LinkedIn | SIH'24 Winner | LinkedIn CoachIn'25 | IGDTUW'27
content:
Every great idea starts somewhere. Ours started as a few sketches on a whiteboard and eventually became something we were proud to present.

I'm incredibly excited to share that our team, Prod Breakers, won the 'Dream Big' category at the LinkedIn India Intern Hackathon 2026!

I never thought that when I walked into LinkedIn for the first time, I'd have the opportunity to build something from scratch, pitch it on stage and celebrate a hackathon win. It's been a reminder of how quickly you can grow when you're challenged, supported and encouraged to dream big.

Over the hackathon, we built Signal, an AI career companion designed to help professionals become more discoverable. From identifying the right people in your network to crafting strategic content and outreach, Signal helps turn career goals into actionable opportunities.

A huge shoutout to my teammates Rahul Raj Reddipogula, B Sai Sri Vardhan, Dhimant Bhuva, Vijay Karthick K. This win is a reflection of the effort, collaboration and creativity we brought together throughout the hackathon.

A big thank you to the organizers, mentors and judges for putting together such an incredible hackathon experience.

Walking away with new learnings, great memories and even more excitement for what's ahead.

#LinkedIn #LinkedInLife #LIET2026 #Hackathon #Innovation

author_headline: Founding Engineer @ LunaBill (YC F25) | math-cs @ UCSD
content:
LeetCode is a ruined platform in 2026.

Did my first contest in a while for fun today. Solved 4/4 in 46 minutes. Finished 586th and LOST rating.

So out of curiosity I checked everyone who beat me. 86% rated below 2400 (all miraculously solving 4/4). 161 are new accounts, and still somehow solved all 4/4 in 20 minutes (we all know how). And that 86% is generous, plenty of the 2400+ accounts are cheating too.

Two years ago this would be a top 100 finish. Today it's 586th because the leaderboard is stuffed with throwaway accounts copy pasting from an LLM in real time.

LeetCode you can obviously see this. There's rating predictor that can flag these accounts performing 500 points above their history in one round. So do something. IP bans, HWID perma bans, anything. People who's done less than 10 contests shouldn't even be on the leaderboard. Or just admit you don't care and let the contests keep dying.

It's genuinely sad watching a platform I used to enjoy go down like this.

author_headline: SDE-1@Amazon || Ex-SDE-1 Intern @Amazon'26 || Former ACON Intern @Microsoft'25 | Amazon ML summer school'24 | CSE(AI&ML)@ CMR College of Engineering & Technology'26
content:
Two internships.

Two incredible learning experiences.

Two full-time offers.

One decision.

I'm grateful to share that I received full-time offers from both organizations where I had the privilege of interning:

Microsoft - Associate Consultant
Amazon - Software Development Engineer I (SDE I)

Each opportunity challenged me in unique ways, helped me grow as an engineer, and gave me the chance to learn from exceptional mentors and teammates. I'm truly thankful to everyone who guided, encouraged, and believed in me throughout this journey.

After careful consideration, I'm excited to share that I've chosen to begin the next chapter of my career as a Software Development Engineer I (SDE I) at Amazon.

A heartfelt thank you to Microsoft and Amazon for the trust, learning, and experiences that have shaped my journey. I'm looking forward to embracing new challenges, continuing to learn, and contributing to meaningful impact.

Here's to new beginnings!

#Amazon #Microsoft #SDE #SoftwareEngineering #CareerMilestone #Internship #FullTimeOffer #NewBeginnings #Gratitude #WomenInTech

author_headline: Co-Founder @Careerflow.ai | Keynote Speaker | Ex-Senior Technical Recruiter @Uber | Ex @Amazon | 496K+ Followers | TEDx Speaker | Daily AI content about job search tips | YouTube
content:
Dear Recruiters & Hiring Managers,
Every resume you open is more than just a list of jobs.
It could be a single mom working late nights just to apply.
It could be a recent grad hoping someone takes a chance.
It could be someone who was laid off, trying to start over.
It could be a dad with a baby to feed and bills piling up.

Behind that one document there's hope, there's pressure, there's a dream waiting to be seen.
You hold more than a candidate in your hands. You hold someone's future, someone's confidence, someone's next chance.

Please don't rush the process. Please don't ghost. Please don't forget: there's a human behind every application.

Let's make hiring kinder. Let's lead with empathy.
